                                            padding-right:0px;

																				<img src="https://my.rcm1.com/files/CMs3wxea07TSWHSxPaZQcbJE9hUXCEYuqUkH8cs0ko3yCm0UQMItX4u1CLqDIs70kjw_gEqXEW25i9UlScrL8vDfBK211_aOWlss_ed7DIT_fz-ILKzNmSsqzGrCLaqhAKCJOX43YWBuqjtuMhKVDw.jpg" border="0" hspace="0" vspace="0" alt="" width="296" height="657" style="max-width:1000px;width:296px;" />
																			</td>
																		</tr>
																	</table>
																</td>
															</tr>
														</table>
														<!--[if mso]>
                    </td>
                    <td valign="top" style="width:60%;">
                    <![endif]
                -->
														<table class="force-row" align="right" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" border="0" style="display:inline-table;float:right;width:451px;">
															<tr>
																<td class="content text" valign="top" style="
                                background-color:transparent;
                                padding-bottom:0px;
                                padding-left:0px;
                                padding-right:0px;
                                padding-top:0px;">
																	<table class="force-row" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" border="0" style="width:100%;">
																		<tr>
																			<td class="section_heading" valign="top" style="
                                                background-color:transparent;
                                                text-align:left;
                                                color:#003f2d;
                                                font-family:Tahoma, sans-serif;
                                                font-style:normal;
                                                font-weight:bold;
                                                font-size:19px;
                                                padding-bottom:0px;
                                                padding-left:0px;
                                                padding-right:0px;
                                                padding-top:0px;
                                                word-break:break-word;
                                            ">Investment Highlights</td>
																		</tr>
																		<tr>
																			<td valign="top" style="
                                                    line-height:5px;
                                                    height:5px;
                                                "> 
                                            </td>
																		</tr>
																		<tr>
																			<td class="copy" valign="top" style="
                                            color:#435254;
                                            font-family:Tahoma, sans-serif;
                                            font-size:13px;
                                        "><div><strong>Fully Renovated 90’s Built Asset Offered at a Low Basis with Strong Initial Yield</strong><br>The Manning is a fully renovated quiet, low density community built in four phases from 1993-1996. Offered at an attractive basis of approximately $88,500 per unit, it provides investors with a stabilized asset well below replacement cost. Based on current operations, the in-place cap rate is strong at 6.6%, reflecting stable cash flow from day one.<br><br><strong>$3 Million+ Invested in Comprehensive Improvements</strong><br>Since 2021, the prior owner and current ownership have invested more than $3 million into The Manning, significantly upgrading interiors, amenities, common areas, and major building systems. Nearly $8,000 per unit has been spent on interior renovations, with 100% of units upgraded since 2024, including LVT flooring in wet areas, granite countertops, painted cabinetry, updated plumbing fixtures, modern lighting, and stainless steel and white appliances. The clubhouse has been fully renovated with a new paint scheme, fitness center, coffee bar, and floor‑to‑ceiling windows overlooking the pool and patio. Additional improvements include fully renovated common hallways, LED exterior lighting, a refreshed exterior paint scheme, asphalt slurry and seal coating, and multiple roof replacements.<br><br><strong>Assumable Existing Debt</strong><br>The offering benefits from an assumable in-place Freddie Mac loan featuring a 5.29% fixed interest rate with an August 2030 maturity, providing investors with immediate clarity around the capital stack and near-term debt service. <em><strong>This favorable existing debt must be assumed and is interest only until September 2028. </strong></em>​​​​​​​<br><br><strong>Upside Through Income & Amenity Enhancements</strong><br>Other‑income upside exists through fully implementing trash, pest control and renters insurance fees, potential increases to under‑market parking rents and implementation of bulk internet service.<br><br>While the asset is substantially renovated and rent premiums have already been realized on upgraded units, additional upside remains. Opportunities include modest amenity additions such as a dog park, grilling areas, and/or a playground to further enhance resident appeal. <br><br><strong>Strategic Evansville Location with Regional Demand Drivers</strong><br>The property is located in Evansville, Indiana, the economic hub for a regional workforce exceeding 300,000 people across Southwest Indiana, Southeast Illinois, and Northwest Kentucky. Proximity to downtown Evansville, major employers (Ascension St. Vincent and University of Evansville), and adjacent green space at Vann Park enhances resident appeal. The market is further supported by identified housing undersupply, strong inbound commuting patterns, and planned infrastructure improvements including the I‑69 Ohio River Crossing, reinforcing long‑term rental demand fundamentals.</div></td>
																		</tr>
																	</table>
																</td>
															</tr>
														</table>

                </tr>
                </table>
                <![endif]-->
													</td>
												</tr>
